evals.name: feature-from-main
evals.prompt: Add a dark-mode toggle to the settings page.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes it's on main, creates a GitHub issue with a full task body, runs a planning->review->approval loop in issue comments, branches feat/<desc> off up-to-date main, commits with a Conventional Commit + Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer, and opens a squash-ready PR that Closes the issue.
assertions: Creates a GitHub issue capturing the task
Issue carries a planning-agent plan comment
Issue carries a review comment with an explicit 'Verdict: APPROVED'
Work branch follows type/description naming (feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test)/...
At least one commit uses Conventional Commit format
A commit includes the Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
A pull request was opened
No GitHub Project was created (only 1 issue, below the >3 threshold)

evals.name: bugfix-flow
evals.prompt: Fix the bug where the cart total shows NaN on an empty cart, it's in src/cart.js.
evals.expected_output: Creates an issue describing the NaN bug, gets a reviewed+approved plan in comments, branches fix/<desc>, fixes src/cart.js, commits as a Conventional Commit with the co-author trailer, opens a squash PR closing the issue.
assertions: Creates a GitHub issue capturing the task
Issue carries a planning-agent plan comment
Issue carries a review comment with an explicit 'Verdict: APPROVED'
Work branch follows type/description naming (feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test)/...
At least one commit uses Conventional Commit format
A commit includes the Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
A pull request was opened
No GitHub Project was created (only 1 issue, below the >3 threshold)

evals.name: existing-issues-to-project
evals.expected_output: Detects there are more than 3 open issues, creates a GitHub Project, adds the open issues to it, then proceeds to work the first issue through the plan/review/implement flow.
assertions: Creates a GitHub Project for the workstream
Adds the repo's issues to the Project
Works an issue through a planning-agent plan comment
Issue carries a review comment with an explicit 'Verdict: APPROVED'
Work branch follows type/description naming
At least one commit uses Conventional Commit format
A commit includes the Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
A pull request was opened

evals.name: negative-readonly
evals.prompt: what's the git command to list my local branches again?
evals.expected_output: Just answers the question (git branch) directly. Does NOT create an issue, branch, project, or PR, and does not spin up the planning/review agents.
assertions: Answers the question directly
Creates NO GitHub issue
Creates NO branch, PR, or Project

evals.name: greenfield-new-project
evals.prompt: I'm kicking off a new note-taking app — I want user auth, a notes CRUD API, and a sync service. Let's get going.
evals.expected_output: Breaks the initiative into 3+ distinct GitHub issues, recognizes that crosses the >3 threshold, creates a new GitHub Project and adds the issues to it, then begins the plan/review/implement flow on the first issue.
assertions: Files 3 or more distinct GitHub issues
Creates a GitHub Project and adds the issues to it
Works the first issue through a planning-agent plan comment
Issue carries a review comment with an explicit 'Verdict: APPROVED'
Work branch follows type/description naming
At least one commit uses Conventional Commit format + Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
A pull request was opened
